name: The CPython GIL affects what?
question_text: In CPython, what does the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) primarily prevent?
general_feedback: The GIL ensures that only one native thread executes Python bytecode at a time, which serialises CPU-bound pure-Python work across threads. I/O-bound threads still benefit from threading because the GIL is released during most I/O waits.
correct_feedback: Correct — The GIL serialises bytecode execution across threads in CPython.

answers.text: Multiple threads from executing Python bytecode simultaneously.
answers.feedback: The GIL serialises bytecode execution across threads in CPython.

answers.text: The interpreter from ever being used in multi-core machines.
answers.feedback: The interpreter runs on multi-core hardware; the GIL affects parallelism within a process, not whether CPython can run.

answers.text: I/O-bound programs from benefiting from threading.
answers.feedback: I/O releases the GIL, so threading is effective for I/O-bound workloads.

answers.text: Multiprocessing from running Python code in parallel.
answers.feedback: multiprocessing spawns separate processes with their own GIL, so they run Python bytecode in parallel.
